title: N.E.W.T.s
prose: N.E.W.T.s make O.W.L.s look like a warm-up: practical demonstrations in front of external examiners, essays that expect you to argue rather than recite, a Charms exam that has you holding a complex piece of magic steady for a full five minutes under someone else's watchful eye.
You walk out of the last exam into a warm June afternoon that feels almost unfairly ordinary, given how much of the last seven years has led to this one string of mornings in the Great Hall.

choices.label: Head down to the lake to wait it out with everyone else.
